steps.title: Find the sentence boundary
steps.content: The opening phrase “By supplying grain, textiles, and tin to societies that lacked these valuable items” is introductory. The main clause begins with “the Neo-Assyrian Empire grew,” so the introductory phrase needs a comma before that clause.

steps.title: Use a comma after the opener
steps.content: Choice A makes the introductory phrase a fragment. Choice B runs the phrase into the main clause with no boundary. Choice C uses a semicolon, but the first part is not an independent clause. A comma after “items” correctly introduces the main clause. The answer is D.
